# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Project Overview
This is a comprehensive Human Resources management frontend application built with React, TypeScript, and Vite. It provides a complete HR solution with role-based access control, connecting to a Supabase backend.

## Architecture & Technology Stack

### Frontend Framework
- **React 18** with functional components and hooks
- **TypeScript** for type safety throughout the application
- **Vite** as the build tool and development server
- **React Router v6** for client-side routing with protected routes

### UI & Styling
- **Tailwind CSS** with custom design tokens
- **shadcn/ui** component library (Radix UI primitives)
- **Lucide React** for consistent iconography
- **CSS custom properties** for theming support

### State Management & Data Fetching
- **React Query (TanStack Query)** for server state management
- **React Hook Form** with Zod validation for forms
- **Context API** for authentication state

### External Integrations
- **Supabase** for backend services (auth, database, real-time)
- **Tiptap** for rich text editing
- **Recharts** for data visualization
- **date-fns** for date manipulation

## Application Structure

### Routing & Pages
The application uses React Router with the following main routes:
- `/login` - Authentication
- `/dashboard` - Main dashboard
- `/employees` - Employee management (CRUD)
- `/projects` - Project management
- `/clients` - Client management
- `/invoices` - Invoice management
- `/hr-policies` - HR policy management
- `/evaluations` - Performance evaluations
- `/payroll` - Payroll processing
- `/settings` - System configuration

### Component Organization
- `src/components/ui/` - Reusable UI primitives (buttons, inputs, etc.)
- `src/components/layout/` - Layout components (AppLayout, sidebar, header)
- `src/components/[feature]/` - Feature-specific components
- `src/pages/` - Route-level page components
- `src/hooks/` - Custom React hooks
- `src/lib/` - Utility functions and business logic

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Authentication & Authorization
- **Supabase Auth** for user authentication
- **Role-based access control** with 5 roles: employee, hr_manager, finance_manager, team_lead, ceo
- **Protected routes** with automatic redirects based on permissions
- **Route-level access control** in `src/lib/role-access.ts`

### Data Flow
- **React Query** for caching and synchronization
- **Optimistic updates** for better UX
- **Real-time subscriptions** via Supabase for live data
- **Form validation** with Zod schemas

### Component Patterns
- **Compound components** for complex UI elements
- **Render props** for flexible component APIs
- **Custom hooks** for shared logic
- **Composition over inheritance** throughout

## Database Schema (Supabase)

### Core Entities
- **employees** - User profiles and organizational data
- **companies** - Multi-tenant company information
- **projects** - Project management
- **clients** - Client relationships
- **invoices** - Billing and invoicing
- **evaluations** - Performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations** - Continuous feedback
- **hr_policies** - Company policies
- **payroll** - Salary and compensation
- **loans** - Employee loan tracking

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies
- Projects have many employees (many-to-many)
- Evaluations reference employees and evaluators
- Invoices link to projects and clients

## Role-Based Permissions

### Employee
- View personal dashboard, projects, evaluations
- Access HR policies and personal payslips
- Submit daily evaluations and loan requests

### Team Lead
- All employee permissions
- Manage team projects and evaluations
- Access team performance data

### HR Manager
- All employee permissions
- Full employee CRUD operations
- Manage evaluations, policies, and holidays
- Access settings and organizational configuration

### Finance Manager
- All employee permissions
- Manage payroll, invoices, and financial reports
- Access expense tracking and financial analytics

### CEO
- Full system access
- All permissions across all modules

## Development Workflow

### Build & Development
- `npm run dev` - Vite dev server with HMR
- `npm run build` - Production build optimization
- `npm run test` - Vitest unit testing
- `npm run lint` - ESLint code quality

### Environment Configuration
- Environment variables prefixed with `VITE_`
- Supabase configuration via environment
- Separate configs for different environments

## Key Files & Directories

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx` - Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx` - Main app component with routing

### Core Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` - Authentication hook
- `src/lib/role-access.ts` - Permission logic
- `src/integrations/supabase/` - Database integration

### UI Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx` - Main layout
- `src/components/ui/` - Design system components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx` - Brand component

## Performance Considerations

### Optimization Techniques
- **Code splitting** via React.lazy for route components
- **Image optimization** with proper sizing
- **Query optimization** with React Query caching
- **Bundle analysis** for build size monitoring

### Best Practices
- **TypeScript strict mode** enabled
- **ESLint** with React and accessibility rules
- **Component composition** for reusability
- **Custom hooks** for logic extraction

## Deployment & Production

### Build Process
- Vite handles bundling and minification
- Static asset optimization
- Environment variable injection

### Supabase Integration
- Row Level Security policies
- Real-time subscriptions
- Authentication flows
- Database migrations

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the codebase architecture, patterns, and implementation details for development and maintenance.
